source_intake_record.raw_question_text: The scatter graph shows the distance run and time taken by five students during a training session. Who ran with the fastest average speed? <image1>

image_dependency: question_text explicitly refers to 'The scatter graph'
the student names alone are insufficient; identifying the fastest runner requires reading plotted distance-time data from the image
multi_step: solver must extract or estimate each student's distance and time from the scatter graph
solver must compare average speeds, which requires combining two quantities per student (distance/time)
there are five students/options, so the task involves comparing multiple candidates rather than reading a single value
verifiability: answer_type is short_text and matches a closed set of 5 choices
registry_passed=true and image asset exists, so the sample is well-formed for downstream checking
the target concept is objective: fastest average speed can be checked from the plotted distance-time pairs
image readability_score=0.8498 supports feasible visual verification, though graph reading may still involve approximation

decision_reasons: image, question text, and short-text answer are all present, so the core semantics are fully supported
initial scores are strong overall: high image dependency, solid multi-step reasoning, and high verifiability
the task is objective and answerable from the provided scatter graph with a closed-set short answer
the only noted risk is approximate value extraction from the scatter plot, which is a manageable quality concern rather than a core asset deficiency
